items.title: Lansing sets July hearing on data center moratorium ordinance
items.dek: A public hearing on an ordinance for a temporary moratorium on new data centers is scheduled for July 13 at Lansing City Hall.
items.findings: The ordinance would add Chapter 878, Sections 878.01-878.03 to the Lansing Codified Ordinances to impose a temporary moratorium on new data centers, per the July 2 public notice | The hearing is scheduled for July 13, 2026 at 7:00 p.m. in the Tony Benavides Lansing City Council Chambers, 10th Floor, Lansing City Hall | Written comments accepted at City Clerk's Office or city.clerk@lansingmi.gov until 5 p.m. day of hearing | Full ordinance text and exact terms remain unavailable in the public notice
items.perspective: The hearing opens public comment on the ordinance; any further readings or votes would follow standard Council process and could affect utility loads and land use in the region.
items.judge_note: Document-based agenda preview for a specific July 13 hearing, though the hearing itself was already mentioned in the live story about the $120M withdrawal.
